evals.prompt: Add MatrixScan Pick to my app. The user will provide the real product list and symbologies later — go with a small placeholder product list and a reasonable default symbology set. File: NonScanditViewController.swift
evals.expected_output: The skill adds the MatrixScan Pick pipeline to the existing ScanViewController: imports ScanditBarcodeCapture, creates the DataCaptureContext, builds BarcodePickSettings with a default symbology set, builds a set of BarcodePickProduct, creates a BarcodePickAsyncMapperProductProvider with a provider delegate that maps scanned payloads to product identifiers, constructs the BarcodePick mode, creates a BarcodePickView and adds it to the view hierarchy, wires the BarcodePickViewUIDelegate finish handler, and drives the start()/pause()/stop() lifecycle. The response also shows the setup checklist (SPM, Info.plist, license key).

assertions.text: import ScanditBarcodeCapture is present
A DataCaptureContext is created with DataCaptureContext(licenseKey:) using the placeholder '-- ENTER YOUR SCANDIT LICENSE KEY HERE --'
BarcodePickSettings is constructed and at least two symbologies are enabled via settings.set(symbology:enabled:)
A set of BarcodePickProduct is built, each constructed with an identifier and a quantityToPick
A BarcodePickAsyncMapperProductProvider is constructed with the products and a providerDelegate

The view controller conforms to BarcodePickAsyncMapperProductProviderDelegate and implements mapItems(_:completionHandler:) returning BarcodePickProductProviderCallbackItem values built with itemData and productIdentifier
The BarcodePick mode is constructed via BarcodePick(context:settings:productProvider:)

A BarcodePickView is constructed with BarcodePickView(frame:context:barcodePick:settings:) (a BarcodePickViewSettings instance) and added to the hierarchy with addSubview (the view does NOT add itself)
barcodePickView.start() is called in viewWillAppear
barcodePickView.pause() is called in viewWillDisappear, and stop() is called only when isMovingFromParent is true
The view controller conforms to BarcodePickViewUIDelegate and implements barcodePickViewDidTapFinishButton(_:); barcodePickView.uiDelegate is set

The view controller conforms to BarcodePickActionListener (implements didPickItem(withData:completionHandler:) and didUnpickItem(withData:completionHandler:), each calling completionHandler(true)) and registers it via barcodePickView.addActionListener(self) — required for picks to actually complete
The existing ScanViewController class is preserved (not renamed or removed); the titleLabel IBOutlet or viewDidLoad title assignment is preserved or the user is told it was kept
The response includes the setup checklist: SPM URL (https://github.com/Scandit/datacapture-spm), NSCameraUsageDescription in Info.plist, replace the license key placeholder
No custom BarcodePickViewHighlightStyleCustomView / highlightStyle customization is added (default highlights are fine for the basic integration)

evals.prompt: Add MatrixScan Pick to my SwiftUI app. File: NonScanditSwiftUIView.swift
evals.expected_output: The skill adds a UIKit view controller with the full MatrixScan Pick pipeline (context, BarcodePickSettings + symbologies, product provider, BarcodePick mode, BarcodePickView added with addSubview, lifecycle, finish-button UI delegate, and BarcodePickActionListener), wraps it in a UIViewControllerRepresentable, and changes the existing SwiftUI ScanView body to use that representable. No Scandit API calls live on the SwiftUI side.

assertions.text: import ScanditBarcodeCapture is present

A UIViewController subclass is added with the full MatrixScan Pick setup: DataCaptureContext, BarcodePickSettings with at least two symbologies enabled via set(symbology:enabled:), a set of BarcodePickProduct (identifier + quantityToPick), BarcodePickAsyncMapperProductProvider with a providerDelegate, BarcodePick(context:settings:productProvider:), BarcodePickView(frame:context:barcodePick:settings:) added with addSubview, and start()/pause()/stop() in viewWillAppear/viewWillDisappear (pause then stop only when isMovingFromParent)
The view controller conforms to BarcodePickAsyncMapperProductProviderDelegate and implements mapItems(_:completionHandler:) returning BarcodePickProductProviderCallbackItem values
The view controller conforms to BarcodePickViewUIDelegate and implements barcodePickViewDidTapFinishButton(_:); barcodePickView.uiDelegate is set

The view controller conforms to BarcodePickActionListener (didPickItem(withData:completionHandler:) and didUnpickItem(withData:completionHandler:), each calling completionHandler(true)) and registers it via barcodePickView.addActionListener(self) — required for picks to complete
A UIViewControllerRepresentable-conforming struct is added that makes/updates the UIKit scan view controller
The existing SwiftUI ScanView body uses the new representable (no longer the plain Text placeholder)
No Scandit API call (DataCaptureContext, BarcodePick, BarcodePickView, etc.) appears inside the SwiftUI View struct body
No Scandit API call appears inside makeUIViewController or updateUIViewController beyond constructing the UIKit view controller
The response includes the setup checklist: SPM URL (https://github.com/Scandit/datacapture-spm), NSCameraUsageDescription in Info.plist, replace the license key placeholder

evals.prompt: Also enable QR codes in my MatrixScan Pick app. File: IntegratedBarcodePickViewController.swift
evals.expected_output: The skill adds settings.set(symbology: .qr, enabled: true) inside setupPicking(), preserves the existing enabled symbologies, and does not touch the context / product provider / mode / view / lifecycle / listeners.

assertions.text: settings.set(symbology: .qr, enabled: true) is present
The existing enabled symbologies (.ean13UPCA, .ean8, .upce, .code128, .code39) are all still enabled
No existing symbology line was removed or replaced

Existing context, productDatabase, BarcodePickAsyncMapperProductProvider, BarcodePick construction, BarcodePickView construction, lifecycle, and all four extensions (delegate, view listener, UI delegate, action listener) are preserved
No duplicate BarcodePick, BarcodePickView, or DataCaptureContext instantiation is introduced

evals.prompt: In my MatrixScan Pick app, Code 39 barcodes are always between 10 and 14 characters. Configure that. File: IntegratedBarcodePickViewController.swift
evals.expected_output: The skill accesses the Code 39 SymbologySettings via settings.settings(for: .code39) and sets activeSymbolCounts to the range 10...14, inside setupPicking(), before constructing BarcodePick.

assertions.text: settings.settings(for: .code39) is called to get the SymbologySettings

activeSymbolCounts is assigned a Set<Int> covering 10 through 14 (e.g. Set(10...14)). NSNumber wrapping must NOT be used — the property's Swift type is Set<Int>, so passing Set<NSNumber> will not compile.
The assignment happens inside setupPicking(), before 'BarcodePick(context:' is constructed
.code39 is still enabled (the new configuration layers on top of the existing settings.set(symbology: .code39, enabled: true))
Existing context, productDatabase, provider, BarcodePick construction, BarcodePickView construction, lifecycle, and extensions are preserved

evals.prompt: Use 4K resolution for the camera in my MatrixScan Pick app. File: IntegratedBarcodePickViewController.swift
evals.expected_output: The skill starts from BarcodePick.recommendedCameraSettings, sets preferredResolution to .uhd4k, and switches the BarcodePickView constructor to the designated initializer that accepts cameraSettings:. Existing pieces are preserved.

assertions.text: BarcodePick.recommendedCameraSettings is used as the starting point (not a fresh CameraSettings())
preferredResolution = .uhd4k is set on those camera settings (or equivalent VideoResolution case for 4K)
BarcodePickView is constructed via the designated initializer BarcodePickView(frame:context:barcodePick:settings:cameraSettings:) passing the configured camera settings
The assignments happen before the BarcodePickView constructor call
Existing symbology configuration, productDatabase, BarcodePick construction, lifecycle, and extensions are preserved

evals.prompt: Show a torch button in my MatrixScan Pick view. File: IntegratedBarcodePickViewController.swift
evals.expected_output: The skill sets viewSettings.showTorchButton = true on the BarcodePickViewSettings before the BarcodePickView is constructed.

assertions.text: viewSettings.showTorchButton = true is set
The assignment is on the BarcodePickViewSettings instance (not on BarcodePickView, and not on BarcodePickSettings)
The assignment happens BEFORE the BarcodePickView(frame:context:barcodePick:settings:) constructor call
Existing BarcodePickView construction, symbology configuration, BarcodePick construction, lifecycle, and extensions are preserved

evals.prompt: Mute both the beep and the vibration in my MatrixScan Pick app. File: IntegratedBarcodePickViewController.swift
evals.expected_output: The skill sets settings.isSoundEnabled = false and settings.isHapticsEnabled = false on BarcodePickSettings (the mode settings, not the view settings) before BarcodePick is constructed. The Swift names use the 'is' prefix because the ObjC getters are isSoundEnabled / isHapticsEnabled.

assertions.text: settings.isSoundEnabled = false is set (Swift name has 'is' prefix; using 'soundEnabled' will fail to compile)
settings.isHapticsEnabled = false is set (Swift name has 'is' prefix and plural 'haptics'; using 'hapticsEnabled' will fail to compile)
Both assignments are on the BarcodePickSettings (mode settings) instance — NOT on BarcodePickViewSettings
Both assignments happen BEFORE the BarcodePick(context:settings:productProvider:) constructor call
Existing symbology configuration, BarcodePick construction, BarcodePickView construction, lifecycle, and extensions are preserved

evals.prompt: What's the difference between barcodePickView.pause() and barcodePickView.stop() in MatrixScan Pick?
evals.expected_output: The skill explains that stop() stops scanning and releases the camera (use when the screen is going away, e.g. when isMovingFromParent in viewWillDisappear), while pause() halts scanning without tearing down the camera (use when scanning should pause but the screen remains). Mentions the recommended pattern of calling pause() in viewWillDisappear and stop() only when isMovingFromParent. Includes a link to the official docs.

assertions.text: The response explains that stop() stops scanning and releases the camera
The response explains that pause() halts scanning WITHOUT tearing down the camera
The response gives a usage example or rule of thumb (e.g. pause when scanning halts but the screen remains; stop when the screen is going away or isMovingFromParent is true)
The response mentions start() as the way to resume scanning after pause
The response does NOT invent methods that are not documented (e.g. resume(), unpause(), continueScanning())
The response includes a link to the Scandit iOS documentation (scandit.com/sdks/ios/matrixscan-pick or scandit.com/data-capture-sdk/ios)
No files are written or edited

evals.prompt:
I have a pick order with three product lines:
- product 'SKU-A' (quantity 5), with two barcodes: 5012345678900 and 5012345678917
- product 'SKU-B' (quantity 2), with one barcode: 5023456789011
- product 'SKU-C' (quantity 1), with one barcode: 5034567890122

Walk me through setting this up in MatrixScan Pick: what goes into the BarcodePickProduct set, what the mapItems delegate needs to do (with the exact identifiers and barcode payloads above), and what the user sees while scanning.
evals.expected_output: The skill walks through the three pieces concretely with the given identifiers and payloads: builds a Set<BarcodePickProduct> with the three products (identifier 'SKU-A' qty 5, 'SKU-B' qty 2, 'SKU-C' qty 1), shows a BarcodePickAsyncMapperProductProvider with a providerDelegate, and writes a mapItems implementation that maps each of the four payloads to its product identifier — with BOTH '5012345678900' and '5012345678917' mapped to 'SKU-A' (demonstrating the many-to-one capability). Explains that unknown payloads are omitted from the returned array and surfaced as not-in-list. Describes what the user sees: highlights drawn over each detected barcode, tapping confirms the pick (which goes through the BarcodePickActionListener), per-product quantities count up toward the goal, and the user can finish via the finish button. Includes a link to the official docs.

assertions.text: Creates BarcodePickProduct(identifier: "SKU-A", quantityToPick: 5)
Creates BarcodePickProduct(identifier: "SKU-B", quantityToPick: 2)
Creates BarcodePickProduct(identifier: "SKU-C", quantityToPick: 1)
Collects the three BarcodePickProducts into a Set<BarcodePickProduct>
Uses BarcodePickAsyncMapperProductProvider(products:providerDelegate:) with the correct parameter label providerDelegate: (not providerCallback:)
The mapItems implementation maps barcode payload '5012345678900' to productIdentifier 'SKU-A'
The mapItems implementation ALSO maps barcode payload '5012345678917' to productIdentifier 'SKU-A' (many-to-one: both SKU-A barcodes resolve to the same product identifier)
The mapItems implementation maps barcode payload '5023456789011' to productIdentifier 'SKU-B'
The mapItems implementation maps barcode payload '5034567890122' to productIdentifier 'SKU-C'
The mapItems signature matches the real delegate method: func mapItems(_ items: [String], completionHandler: @escaping ([BarcodePickProductProviderCallbackItem]) -> Void)
Returned items are BarcodePickProductProviderCallbackItem(itemData: ..., productIdentifier: ...) values
Unknown barcode payloads are omitted from the returned array (the explanation says they surface as 'not-in-list')

The user-facing description mentions highlights drawn over each detected barcode and that tapping a highlight picks it (going through the BarcodePickActionListener / completionHandler(true) confirmation)
The user-facing description mentions that the per-product quantities are tracked by the SDK (e.g. user picks 5 of SKU-A, 2 of SKU-B, 1 of SKU-C) and that a finish button is available
Does not invent APIs that are not documented in the skill (no providerCallback:, no resume()/unpause(), no pick(withItemData:), no BarcodePickAsyncMapperProductProviderCallback)
Includes a link to the Scandit iOS documentation (scandit.com/sdks/ios/matrixscan-pick or scandit.com/data-capture-sdk/ios)
No files are written or edited
